what_it_does: This file is a structured design-system substrate for an 18-page investor/partner-facing company overview deck for ConceptV. It records the narrative arc, attention flow per slide, categorized visual findings with recipes and generator rules, reusable components, and content-length samples.
observations: Documents 18 slides from cover to contact/closer with a declared narrative arc (Aspiration → Problem → Solution → Evidence → Team → Call to action).
Uses a warm off-white background (#FAFAF8 / #F8F6F2) and a single golden accent (#B8960C) across all interior slides.
All headings and labels are required to stay single-line with explicit character budgets.
Arrow bullets replace dot bullets throughout; left-pointing triangles are reserved for image-reference labels.
Hexagon shape is reserved for named system-level concepts (Virtual Tours, Property Wizard, User Portal, ConceptV logo).
Cover and closer slides are full-bleed warm architectural photos that mirror each other.
Before/after network diagrams appear on slides 3 and 9 as the primary argument pattern.
A faint architectural/topographic map watermark is applied to every interior slide at very low contrast.
A small V-mark footer icon is required on every content slide.
CTA buttons are always outlined/ghost, never filled.
Light mode only; no dark-mode slides.
Green is used only for status dots indicating active/live state.
Provides a components catalog (StatOrb, ArrowBulletList, FeatureCard, HexagonBadge, KPIBox, etc.) and content_samples with character budgets.
standouts: The emergent 'strict one-line rule for all headings and labels' with explicit character budgets.
The cover/closer 'emotional bracket' using matched full-bleed warm photography.
The 'diagram-as-argument' pattern where before/after network graphs are the proof, not decoration.
The architectural map watermark as a consistent substrate texture across all interior slides.
conventions: Follows a categorized findings schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule.
Maintains a restrained warm palette: off-white paper, charcoal text, golden accent, no pure black or bright yellow.
Uses consistent geometric vocabulary: hexagons for system names, circles for entities/people, arrows for direction.
Applies light-mode chrome uniformly; dark elements are reserved for data encoding.
Requires every content slide to carry the same footer V-mark anchor.
Expresses spacing as computed relationships (card padding = gutter, title-to-subtitle tighter than title-to-body).
concerns: Many values are approximate (px sizes, color hexes, opacity percentages) without stated source precision.
No original slide images or asset paths are included, so recipes cannot be directly verified from this file alone.
Some 'emergent' claims appear interpretive rather than strictly observable.
The content_samples bullet example is truncated mid-sentence.
Texture findings are split across 'gradient-shading-opacity' and 'texture' categories with overlapping descriptions.
notes: A dense, rule-heavy design audit that tries to turn a pitch deck into a reproducible visual grammar.
questions: Were the color and size values measured from source files or estimated visually?
What are the exact original slide dimensions and the actual map-texture asset?
How were the 'emergent' rules validated against the source deck?
What is the 'Vi' product referenced in the HexagonBadge reuse note?
Is there a companion file containing the actual slide images or exported assets?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a design-system extraction of the stated 18-page ConceptV company overview / investor pitch deck.
apparent_themes: Economy and restraint in text and color
Transformation as a narrative and diagram pattern
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to codify the deck's visual language into reusable generator rules, turning a one-off presentation into an extensible design substrate.
novelty: It treats a slide deck as a code/design substrate with components, recipes, content budgets, and attention-flow annotations, including emergent rules like equal-padding computation and strict one-line headings.
contribution: A comprehensive, categorized specification of the ConceptV deck's visual language — including findings, reusable components, content budgets, and narrative attention flow — that a fused system could use to generate or maintain consistent slides.
summary_for_embedding: A structured design-system ledger documenting the visual grammar, components, content budgets, and narrative flow of an 18-page ConceptV investor pitch deck.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the deck's design rules and reusable patterns so the same visual language can be replicated or extended across future materials.
